mnemonics: BIG K-Maps Are For Simplifying Expressions (BIG: Binary Inputs, Groups of 1s, Implicants, Function Simplification)
keyPoints: Combinational logic outputs depend only on current inputs, not past states
Common building blocks include multiplexers, decoders, and adders
Boolean algebra and Karnaugh maps are used for simplification
commonMistakes: Forgetting to account for don't-care conditions in K-maps
Creating race conditions with cascaded gates (gate delay issues)
Incorrectly grouping cells in Karnaugh maps (groups must be powers of two and rectangular)
